name: immorterm-terminal
description: Immortal terminals that survive VS Code crashes, restarts, and updates. Powered by GNU Screen.

commands.title: ImmorTerm: Set Theme for This Project
ImmorTerm: Set Theme for This Terminal

immorterm.enabled.markdownDescription:
Enable ImmorTerm for this project.

Set to `true` to activate persistent terminals and memory services.
Set to `false` to disable all ImmorTerm features for this workspace.

When not set, you'll be prompted on next activation.

immorterm.terminalMode.description: Which terminal sessions to auto-restore on startup
immorterm.terminalMode.markdownDescription:
Which terminal sessions to auto-restore on startup.

- **ai**: The ImmorTerm terminal (GPU-rendered Rust engine)
- **regular**: Legacy screen sessions from the retired C binary (kept for existing setups)
- **both**: Both

Manual session creation commands always work regardless of this setting.

immorterm.statusBarTheme.description: Color theme for the terminal status bar
immorterm.statusBarTheme.markdownDescription:
**Color theme for the terminal status bar.**

After changing, run **ImmorTerm: Apply Theme** from the Command Palette (Cmd+Shift+P) and open a new terminal.

---

🟣 Purple Haze • 🔵 Ocean Depths • 🌌 Aurora Borealis • 🟠 Sunset Glow

☀️ Solar Flare • 🏔️ Glacier • 🩷 Rose Gold • 💜 Cyberpunk

⬛ Monochrome Dark • ⬜ Monochrome Light • 🏙️ Neon Tokyo • 🧛 Dracula

💊 Matrix • 📼 Vaporwave • 🔥 Ember • ⚡ Electric Lime

🌊 Tidal • 🟡 Amber • 🌆 Synthwave • 🌋 Molten

🌈 Rainbow

immorterm.scrollbackBuffer.description: Number of lines to keep in Screen scrollback buffer
immorterm.scrollbackBuffer.markdownDescription:
Number of lines to keep in Screen scrollback buffer.

**Higher values** use more memory but preserve more terminal history.

**Example**: `100000` for extensive logs, `10000` for minimal memory usage.

immorterm.historyOnAttach.description: Number of lines to display when reattaching to a Screen session
immorterm.historyOnAttach.markdownDescription:
Number of lines to display when reattaching to a Screen session.

This is the **visible history** shown immediately after VS Code restarts.

**Example**: `5000` for quick startup, `50000` for full history.

immorterm.terminalRestoreDelay.description: Delay in milliseconds between terminal restorations
immorterm.terminalRestoreDelay.markdownDescription:
Delay in milliseconds between terminal restorations.

**Increase** if terminals fail to restore properly on slower systems.

**Example**: `500` for slower machines, `100` for fast SSDs.

immorterm.restoreOnStartup.description: Automatically restore terminals when VS Code opens
immorterm.restoreOnStartup.markdownDescription:
Automatically restore terminals when VS Code opens.

When **enabled**, all previously registered terminals will be recreated and reattached to their Screen sessions.

Set to `false` to manually trigger restoration via the Command Palette.

immorterm.maxLogSizeMb.description: Maximum total size of log files in megabytes
immorterm.maxLogSizeMb.markdownDescription:
Maximum **total** size of all log files in megabytes before cleanup.

When exceeded, the oldest logs are removed to free space.

**Example**: `100` for limited disk space, `1000` for extensive logging.

immorterm.logRetainLines.description: [Reserved] Number of lines to retain when truncating log files
immorterm.logRetainLines.markdownDescription:
⚠️ **Reserved for future use** — this setting is not yet wired into the cleanup logic.

Number of lines to retain when truncating large log files.

Keeps the **most recent** lines, removing older content.

**Example**: `10000` for minimal logs, `100000` for detailed history.

immorterm.enableDebugLog.markdownDescription:
Enable verbose debug logging to the **ImmorTerm** output channel.

Useful for troubleshooting issues. View logs via:
- `View` > `Output` > Select **ImmorTerm** from dropdown

immorterm.closeGracePeriod.description: Grace period before cleaning up a closed terminal (ms)
immorterm.closeGracePeriod.markdownDescription:
Grace period in milliseconds before cleaning up a closed terminal's Screen session.

**Purpose**: Prevents accidental cleanup during VS Code window reloads.

**Example**: `30000` (30 seconds) for faster cleanup, `120000` (2 minutes) for safety.

immorterm.autoCleanupStale.description: Automatically cleanup orphaned Screen sessions on startup
immorterm.autoCleanupStale.markdownDescription:
Automatically cleanup stale Screen sessions on extension activation.

**Removes**: Orphaned sessions without corresponding VS Code terminals.

Set to `false` to manually manage cleanup via Command Palette.

immorterm.statusBarEnabled.description: Show ImmorTerm status in the status bar
immorterm.statusBarEnabled.markdownDescription:
Show ImmorTerm status in the VS Code status bar.

**Displays**:
- Terminal count
- Screen availability status
- Warning icon if Screen is missing

Click the status item to view detailed status.

immorterm.namingPattern.markdownDescription:
Pattern for auto-naming new terminals.

**Available variables**:
- `${project}` - Workspace folder name (lowercase)
- `${n}` - Sequential number

**Examples**:
- `immorterm-${n}` → `immorterm-1`, `immorterm-2` (default)
- `${project}-${n}` → `myapp-1`, `myapp-2`
- `Terminal ${n}` → `Terminal 1`, `Terminal 2`

Note: The status bar already shows `{project} / {tab-name}`, so the default doesn't include project name.

immorterm.closeExistingOnRestore.description: Close existing terminals before restoring saved terminals
immorterm.closeExistingOnRestore.markdownDescription:
Close all existing terminals before restoring saved terminals on startup.

**When enabled (default)**: Provides a clean slate - all open terminals are closed, then saved terminals are restored.

**When disabled**: Saved terminals are restored alongside any already-open terminals.

immorterm.claudeAutoResume.description: Automatically resume Claude Code sessions after restart
immorterm.claudeAutoResume.markdownDescription:
Automatically resume Claude Code sessions after VS Code restart.

**Requires**: Claude Code extension to be installed.

When enabled, ImmorTerm will attempt to restore Claude session context.

immorterm.claudeSyncInterval.description: Claude session sync interval in milliseconds
immorterm.claudeSyncInterval.markdownDescription:
Interval in milliseconds for syncing Claude session state.

**Lower values** provide more frequent saves but may impact performance.

**Example**: `10000` (10s) for frequent saves, `60000` (1 min) for less overhead.

immorterm.claudeIdleTimeout.description: [Reserved] Kill idle Claude sessions after this many hours (0 = never)
immorterm.claudeIdleTimeout.markdownDescription:
⚠️ **Reserved for future use** — this setting is not yet implemented.

Automatically terminate Claude sessions with no terminal I/O for this many hours.

**How it works**: Uses terminal log file mtime as the activity signal. When idle time exceeds the threshold, Claude receives SIGTERM (graceful), then SIGKILL after 5s. The screen session stays alive — you can always resume with `claude --resume <UUID>`.

A beautiful hibernation message with your theme colors is displayed in the terminal.

Set to `0` to disable.

immorterm.claudeChannelsEnabled.description: Enable interactive session sharing via Claude Code channels
immorterm.claudeChannelsEnabled.markdownDescription:
Enable **interactive session sharing** between ImmorTerm AI terminals.

When enabled, the `claude` command is wrapped to include a channel server that allows two Claude sessions to exchange messages in real time (via drag-and-drop Interactive mode).

Disable this if you experience issues or prefer static-only session sharing.

immorterm.closeAction.description: What happens when a terminal tab is closed
immorterm.closeAction.markdownDescription:
What happens when a terminal tab is closed.

- **shelve** (default): Detach the session — it stays alive in the background and can be reattached later via **ImmorTerm: Reattach Terminal**.
- **kill**: Permanently destroy the session (old behavior).

The **Forget** command always kills, regardless of this setting.

immorterm.shelvedSessionTtl.description: Hours before shelved sessions are automatically reaped
immorterm.shelvedSessionTtl.markdownDescription:
Hours before shelved sessions are automatically reaped.

Shelved sessions older than this TTL are archived and killed.

**Range**: 1–168 hours (1 hour to 1 week)

**Default**: 24 hours

immorterm.softShelveEnabled.description: Keep AI daemons alive briefly after closing a tab for instant reattach
immorterm.softShelveEnabled.markdownDescription:
Keep AI daemons alive briefly after closing a tab so reattach is **instant** (no scrollback replay, no Claude `--resume`).

- **false** (default): closing a tab triggers full shelve — daemon SIGTERM + archive.
- **true**: closing a tab marks the daemon as 'soft shelved' for `softShelveTtlMinutes`. Within that window, reattach is a single WebSocket reconnect. After TTL expires, the regular shelve flow runs.

Tradeoff: each idle soft-shelved daemon costs ~20–50 MB RAM. Forgotten sessions accumulate until TTL.

immorterm.softShelveTtlMinutes.description: Minutes a soft-shelved daemon stays alive before falling through to regular shelve
immorterm.softShelveTtlMinutes.markdownDescription:
Minutes a soft-shelved daemon stays alive before falling through to regular shelve.

**Range**: 1–1440 minutes (1 minute to 24 hours)

**Default**: 120 minutes (2 hours)

Only used when `immorterm.softShelveEnabled` is true.

immorterm.screenBinary.description: Path to the GNU Screen binary
immorterm.screenBinary.markdownDescription:
Path to the GNU Screen binary.

**Default**: `immorterm` (ImmorTerm's patched version with UTF-8 title support)

**Install**: download from GitHub Releases

**Fallback**: Set to `screen` to use the standard GNU Screen (some features may not work correctly)

immorterm.services.memory.enabled.description: Enable persistent memory for Claude Code
immorterm.services.memory.enabled.markdownDescription:
Enable persistent memory for Claude Code.

**Provides**:
- Semantic search for past decisions and context
- Memory of architectural choices across sessions
- Context loading at session start
- Code change tracking with diffs and provenance

**Requirements**: Docker must be installed and running

**Resources**: ~500 MB RAM (Docker containers)

immorterm.services.mcpGateway.enabled.description: Enable MCP Gateway to reduce memory usage by ~90%
immorterm.services.mcpGateway.enabled.markdownDescription:
Enable MCP Gateway to reduce memory usage by ~90%.

**Problem**: Each Claude session spawns all MCP servers (context7, tavily, etc.) as separate processes. With 10+ sessions, this consumes 10+ GB of RAM.

**Solution**: The gateway runs all MCP servers as shared singleton processes and exposes them via HTTP. Claude sessions connect to the gateway instead of spawning their own processes.

**Memory impact**: ~10 GB → ~1 GB (90% reduction)

**Requirements**: `npm install -g immorterm-mcp-gateway`

immorterm.services.graph.enabled.description: Enable relationship tracking for Claude Code
immorterm.services.graph.enabled.markdownDescription:
Enable relationship tracking for Claude Code (Neo4j).

**Provides**:
- Cause→effect chains for decisions
- File dependency tracking
- Temporal reasoning

**Power feature** for complex projects.

**Resources**: ~1-2 GB additional RAM
